calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-03-2021 Continued To Feb-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-04-2021 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Continued To Jan-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz, Esq., counsel for David Levin, Trustee (appearing by video conference).

Mr. Schultz advises that the accounting was e-filed yesterday but has not yet shown up on the Register of Actions, requesting a brief continuance for the same to be filed.
The Court continues the matter for status of the accounting to January 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If the accounting is filed one week prior to that date, the matter will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-05-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Daivd Levin and Gene Marchi.

Mr. Schultz stated that nobody is objecting, that he was told by Attorney Thomas Cain to represent to the Court that he supports the fee request, that there is a growing elderly community, and that there is a large market for and need of fiduciary services. Mr. Schultz also spoke of the standard 1% guideline fee and the costs of doing business in the Bay Area, stated that Mr. Levin has a full staff, that he pays rent, that they have someone who does probate accountings, that $145.00 an hour does not cover their overhead and allow them to make a living, and that they also have to be insured. In addition, Mr. Schultz spoke of Mr. Marchi and Mr. Levin's level of education, expertise and level of skill. Mr. Levin spoke of the need to be fully staffed to meet the needs of the public requiring their services. The Third Account and Report of Co-Trustees, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Gene Marchi.

Mr. Schultz addressed the Court's concern with regard to additional fees being paid to the real estate attorney without Court approval stating that it was an oversight on their part, and that his Client has some Court supervised cases and some that are not supervised. Mr. Schultz also stated that Mr. Conrad sends monthly invoices, that the bills were paid as they came in, that his Client's office did not realize Court supervision was required in this case, that his Client's office procedures have been changed and that Mr. Conrad's services were very valuable to the Trust. The Court granted the Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order), but the Court is not approving the actions of the Trustee with regard to the overpayment.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
